description_long: This is a vintage gramophone-style record player featuring a large, flared brass horn with a weathered patina, mounted on a dark wooden base. The horn is attached to a metal arm that curves down to the turntable, which holds a black vinyl record with a white center label. The wooden base is rectangular with decorative molding and small feet, and it houses a hand-crank mechanism on one side for manual operation. The tonearm, also metallic, is positioned over the record and appears to be fixed in place. The overall appearance is antique, with visible signs of wear and age, making it a striking and nostalgic object suitable for display or use in a period urban setting.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, dark wooden base, and hand-crank mechanism, showing signs of age and wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, flared opening of the brass horn and the top of the wooden base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the horn, the hand-crank, and the vinyl record on the turntable.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the horn, the record, and the full rectangular shape of the wooden base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the horn's curve, the tonearm, and the record, with the hand-crank less visible.
attribute_turntable_platter: Present and visible, the turntable platter is circular and black, appearing to be made of vinyl or a similar material. It is proportionally small compared to the large horn, with a white center label and subtle grooves indicating where the record sits. The platter is mounted on the wooden base and is slightly recessed.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, metallic, and curved, extending from the side of the base toward the center of the platter. It is medium in length, appears to be made of steel or brass, and is positioned over the record, suggesting it is ready to play.
